source_jobs.title: Head Of Performance Creative
source_jobs.description:
About us

inBeat is a performance creative agency. We build the UGC, statics, and motion creative that drives results for brands like P&G, NielsenIQ, Linktree, Bumble, New Balance, Hopper, and more. We are known for spotting concepts that scale - the street-interview format that turned ClearScore into a long-running franchise, the podcast format that broke open Growth Therapy - and turning them into repeatable home runs.

We grew up as a UGC and influencer agency. Performance creative - particularly statics and the systems that turn winners into franchises - is our next chapter. This role is the architect of that chapter.

Role

This is a full-time, remote-first leadership role with global reach. You'll own creative strategy, UGC, statics, video, and motion end-to-end across our roster - and you'll lead the team that delivers it. You'll own ideation, execution, measurement, iteration, and scale.

What you’ll do

 - The full performance creative function. Lead creative strategy, performance design, UGC, statics, video, and motion. Set the bar for taste, speed, and outcomes across every asset and every channel.

 - A team of senior leads - and their growth. Your direct reports include leads across Creative Strategy, Design, Video, and UGC. You won't just manage them; you'll grow them into stronger leaders. You'll inherit multiple departments, and we need someone who has built and developed second-line leadership before.

 - Creative systems that scale. Build frameworks for testing, iteration, learning, and creative development. Partner with Creative Ops, Paid Media, and PM to turn strategy into infrastructure that outlives any individual hero.

 - A senior review layer. You're the last set of eyes before client delivery - catching structural, brief, and copy issues early so we don't have harder conversations later.

 - The paid-creative feedback loop. Mine insights from Motion, Foreplay, and Meta Ads Manager alongside our paid team and pull them back into briefs, hooks, and concepts. Break the silo between paid media and creative.

 - Statics as a real channel. Help us package, price, and produce statics in a way that turns them from a deliverable into a performance lever clients want to buy in volume.

 - Scaled-ad celebration and learning. Identify wins, document them, feed them into sales material and case studies. The video editor, strategist, and creator who made the win all get the shout-out.

 - Operating cadence. Weekly creative syncs, bi-weekly workshops with rotating leadership ownership, monthly KPI reviews, and a scaled-ad celebration channel that reaches the whole team.

What we’re looking for

 - You think in systems and build them without overcomplicating the work.

 - You have run performance creative in a high-volume agency environment - UGC, statics, and motion at scale across multiple accounts.

 - You read CPA, CPM, hook rate, hold rate, and scaled-ad volume fluently - and you know when each one matters and when it doesn't.

 - You've hired and coached creative talent, and you're comfortable making the tough calls when standards aren't being met.

 - You have grown leaders, not just managed ICs. You know the difference.

 - You have strong creative instincts and a sharp eye for taste. You watch winning ads and want to figure out why they work.

 - You're direct, high-context, and comfortable pushing back when you disagree.

 - You have a real opinion on statics as a performance channel and ideas about how to package them at volume.

Must haves

 - 8+ years in performance creative, ideally including time at a top-tier UGC, paid social, or DR creative shop.

 - 4+ years leading creative teams of 10+ people, with both manager and senior IC chapters on your résumé.

 - Track record on Meta and TikTok performance creative - direct experience producing UGC and statics that have scaled to material spend.

 - Deep fluency in Motion (creative analytics), Foreplay (swipe and inspiration), and Meta Ads Manager - and the

source_jobs.title: Head of Creative Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

inBeat is a performance creative agency. We sit inside the Fieldtrip portfolio alongside Content Allies, Creative Milkshake, and AAG, and we build campaigns that move numbers for consumer and B2B brands across Canada and the US.

We are growing fast. The delivery org is scaling hard. And operations need someone who can build the systems that hold all of that together without breaking



THE ROLE

We're looking for a Head of Creative Ops to build and run the operational backbone of inBeat's entire delivery function. From designing airtight systems for our UGC and influencer teams to building the infrastructure that lets our video editors, designers, paid media specialists, and creative strategists do their best work - you'll be the person who makes operations something our team is proud of.

You'll inherit a fast-growing agency (200+ people, scaling to 300 - 400+ in 2027) with a mix of systems and tools that have evolved quickly but not always cleanly. Your job is to bring clarity, consistency, and scalable structure - without killing what makes us move fast.

You'll report to our COO, work hand-in-hand with our Chief Creative Officer, and build a team of 2–3 Operations Managers alongside you.

This is a full-time, fully remote position that allows you to work from anywhere.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Audit, consolidate, and rebuild inBeat's operational systems across 7 delivery functions: UGC, Video Editing, Design, Influencer Marketing, Creative Strategy & Copywriting, Paid Media, and Project Management

 - Build and lead a team of 2–3 Operations Managers embedded across those delivery functions

 - Design and implement scalable workflows, documentation, and tooling that grow with the team.

 - Own and standardize inBeat's operational tech stack (Airtable, ClickUp, Notion, Make/N8N/Zapier, Claude, Fathom, G-Suite), making clear what tools we use, how we use them, and where

 - Work closely with department leads (UGC, PM, Performance Creative, Influencer Marketing, Paid Media, Video Editing, Design) to understand their operational pain points and translate them into systems that solve real problems

 - Build intake, handoff, QA, and escalation processes that keep 100+ team members aligned without requiring constant management overhead

 - Create documentation and training resources that make new hires faster to onboard and easier to get up to speed

 - Identify and eliminate operational bottlenecks across the delivery org before they become fires
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Proven experience leading Creative Ops or Agency Operations at a sizeable agency (100+ people minimum)

 - Deep understanding of how creative delivery functions work - UGC, video, design, paid media, influencer - not just one or two of them

 - Track record of building systems and processes that teams actually use and love.

 - High fluency across project management and ops tooling - Airtable, ClickUp, Notion, Make/Zapier/N8N or equivalents

 - Systems thinker who can see the full operational picture and translate it into clear, well-documented processes

 - Strong communicator who can build trust and buy-in with department leads across different creative disciplines

 - Comfortable operating in high-growth, ambiguous environments - you can build structure without needing everything figured out first
   


WHILE NOT REQUIRED, IT'S AN ADDED PLUS IF YOU ALSO HAVE

 - Experience with backend finance ops, HR ops, or IT ops at an agency

 - Familiarity with AI-assisted operations tooling

 - Experience scaling an agency through the 100 to 200+ headcount range

 - Background in performance marketing, influencer marketing, or paid media operations




WHY JOIN US?

 - Low bureaucracy. High ownership. You will not be waiting for permission to fix things

 - Fully remote (with the possibility to work from anywhere)

 - Flexible hours

 - Unlimited Time Off & Sick Days

 - Five fast-growing companies under one portfolio

 - 200+ team members globally

 - Wo

source_jobs.title: Senior IT Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Fieldtrip is a group of creative growth agencies, led by inBeat, that connects enterprise brands with the creators and communities who move their audiences. We run performance-driven paid media and micro-influencer campaigns for more than 250 brands — powered by a distributed team of roughly 200 people across 15+ countries, and a stack of tools that has scaled faster than the function that supports it.

We are a remote-first, contractor-heavy, data-led agency group. We move quickly, we ship real work for real clients, and we care about the infrastructure behind the work as much as the work itself.



THE ROLE

This is the first dedicated IT leadership role across the portfolio. You are not inheriting a function. You are building one.

You will own IT end-to-end for ~200 people across 5+ agencies: the tools, the identity layer, the vendor stack, the BYOD posture, and the baseline cybersecurity and compliance that unblocks enterprise client deals. Daily support volume is low, around two access requests a day. This is not a helpdesk job. It is a builder's job with a clear growth track toward leadership.

You will partner with the COO, the founders, and the operations team to design an IT function that scales with future acquisitions and gets Fieldtrip ready for SOC 2 Type II and GDPR-grade governance without bloating the team or the budget.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the IT function end-to-end. Systems, identity, access, onboarding, offboarding, tooling, and vendor relationships across every portfolio company.

 - Design for BYOD and distributed teams. Build a security posture that works for personal devices, contractor workflows, and cross-border data realities.

 - Rationalise the stack. Kill shadow IT, consolidate overlapping SaaS tools, and deliver visible monthly savings on platforms like Figma, Notion, Airtable, Adobe Suite and their cousins.

 - Stand up baseline security. MFA, SSO, endpoint protection, incident response, and a living risk register aligned to NIST CSF or ISO 27001.

 - Get us client-ready. Microsoft 365 setup that just works, enterprise security questionnaires answered without scrambling, and a trajectory toward SOC 2 Type II and GDPR-grade data handling.

 - Lead, write, document. Turn tribal knowledge into runbooks. Explain IT risk and tradeoffs in plain English. Build the function so it doesn't depend on you.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


MUST-HAVES

 - 5+ years of hands-on IT ownership at a remote-first Startup or Scale-up, ideally a marketing or creative agency, or a tech startup.

 - Experience building an IT function from scratch, or meaningfully restructuring one.

 - Deep, practical BYOD experience securing a distributed, contractor-heavy workforce.

 - Strong identity and access management foundation (Azure AD + Google Workspace).

 - A builder mindset. You ship systems that run without you.

 - Applied experience with NIST CSF, ISO 27001, or SOC 2 type II + working knowledge of GDPR / CCPA.

 - Excellent written communication. Documentation is a first-class deliverable here.


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Familiarity with POPIA (South Africa) and or PIPEDA (Canada).

 - CISSP, CISM, CompTIA Security+, or equivalent certification (not required).

 - Experience handling enterprise security questionnaires and client procurement alignment.

 - Prior portfolio / multi-entity IT experience.

 - Awareness of CASL as it relates to influencer and campaign outreach.


NOT A GREAT FIT IF

 - Your experience is tied to a helpdesk ticket queue.

 - You come from a 10,000+ person corporate IT department and haven't worked in a high-paced startup environment

 - You want to advise and move on. This is an ownership role for someone looking for a long-term career growth track.


WHY JOIN US

 - You build the function. No legacy system to maintain. No politics to dismantle. A clean sheet and real leadership scope.

 - Real leverage. Every system you design touches ~200 people across 15+ countries from day one.

 - Cle

source_jobs.title: Lead Influencer Manager
source_jobs.description:
About us

We’re a tight-knit team of creative, data-driven people, continuously building services & partnering with the world’s largest brands.

We value great work with talented, innovative & diverse, making meaningful connections with like-minded creatives along the way!

Role

We’re looking for a Lead Influencer Manager to drive influencer strategy and execution across high-impact global client accounts and lead the team.

You will own net-new and strategic accounts end-to-end, lead and develop junior members, and work closely with leadership to ensure the influencer function runs smoothly, predictably, and continues to scale through strong team performance.

What you’ll do

 - Strategy & Creative Leadership
   
   Develop influencer and content strategies tailored to client objectives, defining formats, creator profiles, and creative direction that drive performance.

 - Team Management & Enablement
   
   Lead a team of Mid-level and Junior Influencer Managers, providing direct oversight, coaching, and mentorship. Own their performance and growth while ensuring the team consistently delivers high-quality results across accounts.

 - Campaign Ownership
   
   Lead the full influencer campaign lifecycle from brief to launch, optimization, and results analysis across multiple accounts.

 - Client Relations
   
   Serve as the day-to-day point of contact for assigned accounts, guiding clients through strategy, creator selection, and execution.

 - Sales Support & Proposal Development
   
   Support new business and account growth by building influencer strategies for proposals, contributing to pricing and deliverable structuring, and participating in client pitches and workshops. Help translate client needs into clear influencer scopes and set realistic expectations from the start.

 - Content & Quality Control
   
   Own influencer content approvals, ensuring quality, compliance, and brand alignment before launch.

 - Performance & Optimization
   
   Continuously optimize creator mix, formats, and execution to deliver against awareness, traffic, and conversion goals.

 - Airtable & Ops Ownership
   
   Own and improve the influencer operations system. Evolve Airtable structures, introduce automations, and build simple workflows and documentation to keep campaigns organized, scalable, and easy to manage.

 - Leadership Collaboration
   Work closely with the Head of Influencer Marketing and leadership team to help shape best practices for influencer execution

 - Risk Management & Communication
   Proactively surface risks, blockers, and trade-offs early, clearly documenting decisions and recommendations for both clients and internal teams.

What we’re looking for

 - 5+ years in influencer marketing with a proven track record of delivering successful influencer campaigns

 - Ability to translate client briefs into influencer strategies, creative direction, and execution plans

 - Strong instincts for content formats, creator-brand fit, and what converts across platforms

 - Comfortable working in structured systems (Airtable, Notion, or similar) and maintaining clean workflows

 - Confident, clear communicator with senior stakeholders, especially US-based clients. Fluent English is a must

 - Able to guide and review work from Mid-level and Junior IMs while remaining hands-on in execution

Who You Are

Seasoned influencer marketer who can take full ownership of complex client work and consistently make smart, strategic decisions about creators, content, and performance. You should be comfortable guiding others, working closely with clients and leadership, and spotting issues before they become problems, all while keeping campaigns moving toward strong results.

Why Join inBeat

 - Fast-growing agency with tier-one clients

 - Culture built on autonomy, transparency, and high performance

 - Unlimited Time Off & Sick Days

 - Flexible working hours

 - Fully remote (with the possibility to work from anywhere)

 - High opportu

source_jobs.title: Senior Creative Project Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

We’re a tight-knit team of creative, data-driven people, continuously building services & partnering with the world’s largest brands. We value great work with talented, innovative & driven people, making meaningful connections with like-minded creatives along the way.

If you’re someone who thrives in dynamic environments, enjoys solving complex challenges, and wants to make a real impact, we’d love to meet you!


THE ROLE

The Senior Creative Project Manager leads large, multi-layered client programs with multiple SOWs, workstreams, and deliverables — UGC and Influencer Marketing projects. This is not a traditional PM role - it requires navigating high complexity, creator-driven workflows, and fast-moving priorities.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead and manage enterprise-level client accounts across multiple SOWs, ensuring strong, long-term relationships built on trust and consistent delivery.

 - Serve as the primary client-facing point of contact — owning communication, expectations, and strategic alignment across all programs.

 - Oversee end-to-end UGC and Influencer Marketing projects.

 - Ensure all projects are delivered on time and on budget.

 - Coordinate across Paid Media, Creative, Strategy, and Project Management teams.

 - Source and manage external partners, creators, and influencers when needed.

 - Track budgets, risks, and overall program performance.

 - Support and guide Project Managers as needed.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years in Program, Project, or Account Management within creative, marketing, agency, or content-production environments — with a track record of long tenure and sustained client relationships.

 - Hands-on experience managing UGC and Influencer Marketing projects is required — this is core to the role.

 - Proven success managing enterprise accounts with multi-SOW or multi-stream programs (not just individual projects).

 - Exceptional client-facing communication skills in English — written and verbal — with the ability to present confidently, manage expectations, and build lasting partnerships.

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity and building structure where needed.

 - Strategic thinker with an operational mindset and high attention to detail.

 - Familiarity with project management tools (Asana, Monday.com http://Monday.com, ClickUp, Smartsheet, etc.)


WHY US

 - Unlimited Time Off & Sick Days

 - Flexible hours

 - Fully remote (with the possibility to work from anywhere)

 - High opportunity for growth!

source_jobs.title: Senior Creative QA Specialist
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR QUALITY ASSURANCE SPECIALIST

Most agencies treat QA as a step. We're hiring someone to make it a discipline.

This is inBeat's first dedicated QA hire. You'll build the function from the ground up — designing the review processes, running the day-to-day checks, and over time scaling the practice beyond a single person. The brief is simple: nothing outbound goes to a client or a platform without a clean pass through QC.

The work matters because the mistakes it prevents are the ones that quietly erode trust. A CTA placed where Facebook's button covers it. A caption that crops behind TikTok's UI. A claim on a pet health campaign that drifted from "supports" to "cures" between draft three and final delivery. Small things. Compounding consequences.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

You'll be the last line of defence on outbound creative — copy decks, scripts, captions, assets — before any of it reaches a client or goes live. That means accuracy, brand compliance, regulatory claims, and platform specs, every time. The avoidable issues that make agencies look amateur are the ones you catch first.

The immediate focus is our Tier 1, high-compliance accounts, starting with NutriMax. You'll partner with Valen to build a tiered client framework (low, medium, high compliance) and apply rigorous claim-checking on regulated work — pet health, supplements, and other categories where the difference between "supports" and "cures" isn't semantic, it's legal. You'll maintain claim libraries, do-not-say lists, and approved phrase banks for each Tier 1 brand.

Brand standards sit with you too. Logos, colour, typography, tone of voice — checked against each client's guidelines, not from memory. You'll keep brand standards summaries live for the top accounts so the studio has one reference point, not five.

Then you'll turn all of that into a system. Checklists, review templates, SOPs, pre-flight checks by output type. QA stops being a person and becomes a process the agency can scale. You'll track error patterns, report trends to leadership monthly, and recommend workflow changes that reduce revision cycles before they become a problem.

You'll work across creative, strategy, account management, and PM — the calm, constructive voice in the room who catches problems early without becoming a bottleneck. As volume grows, you'll help coach junior QA or proofreaders, and define what the QA support layer looks like once the load justifies it.



WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU

 - Significant hands-on QA, proofreading, or compliance experience in an agency, regulated industry, or high-volume creative environment

 - A working knowledge of regulatory frameworks for at least one compliance-sensitive category (FDA, FTC, pet health, supplements, finance, or similar)

 - Sharp eye for brand standards — logos, colour, typography, tone of voice — across multiple clients held in parallel

 - Strong working knowledge of platform specs across Meta, TikTok, YouTube, and the static and video formats they accept

 - Proven ability to build process from scratch: SOPs, checklists, review templates, claim libraries

 - Comfortable with high volume and tight turnaround without letting standards slip

 - Clear, constructive communicator — able to flag issues without slowing the work down or putting people on the defensive

 - Highly organised, systematic, and able to hold multiple Tier 1 accounts in your head at once


BONUS, NOT REQUIRED

 - Experience writing or refining brand voice and tone guidelines

 - Familiarity with project management tooling and how QA hands off cleanly to PM

 - Experience onboarding or coaching junior QA or proofreaders

 - A background in copy or editorial — the kind that catches the drift before it shows up in a claim


WHY INBEAT

inBeat is a tight-knit team of creative, data-driven people building services for some of the largest brands in the world. The work is real, the clients are tier-one, and the culture is built on three things: autonomy, transpare

source_jobs.title: Senior UGC Creator Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About us

We’re a tight-knit team of creative, data-driven people, continuously building services & partnering with the world’s largest brands.

We value great work with talented, innovative & driven people, making meaningful connections with like-minded creatives along the way!

Role

We’re looking for a Senior UGC Creator Marketing Manager to own creator campaigns end-to-end—from sourcing and communication to final delivery—ensuring everything is completed on time and aligned with the brief.

You’ll work closely with the creative team to align on content direction and with project managers to keep timelines on track. You’ll also support and guide UGC Coordinators by delegating tasks and stepping in when needed.

We’re looking for someone who is organized, proactive, a strong communicator, and comfortable owning outcomes, not just tasks.

This is a full-time, fully remote position that allows you to work from anywhere.

What you’ll do

 - Own end-to-end execution of UGC campaigns from creator sourcing to final delivery

 - Identify, vet, and secure creators based on campaign needs and brand fit

 - Manage all creator communication, including outreach, follow-ups, and issue resolution

 - Ensure creators deliver content on time and according to the brief

 - Collaborate with project managers to ensure smooth execution and timely delivery

 - Work closely with and manage UGC Coordinators to ensure high-quality execution, strong campaign outcomes, and timely delivery

 - Keep internal systems clean and up to date

 - Oversee creator payments and agreements to ensure accuracy and completion

What we’re looking for

 - Strong written and verbal English communication skills

 - Prior experience in UGC or influencer marketing campaigns

 - Highly organized and able to manage multiple tasks and campaigns at once

 - Tech-savvy and quick to adapt to new tools and platforms

 - Proactive, self-motivated, and able to work independently

 - Strong team player with excellent interpersonal and collaboration skills

 - Comfortable working with cross-functional teams and taking direction when needed

 - Strong attention to detail, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks

 - Capability to stay consistent, reliable, and deliver results in a fast-paced environment

While not required, it’s an added plus if you also have:

 - Familiarity with tools like ClickUp, Frame.io http://Frame.io, or similar

 - Experience in fast-paced agency or startup environments

Why join us?

 - Unlimited Time Off & Sick Days

 - Flexible hours

 - Fully remote (with the possibility to work from anywhere)

 - High opportunity for growth!

source_jobs.title: UGC Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
About us

We’re a tight-knit team of creative, data-driven people, continuously building services & partnering with the world’s largest brands.

We value great work with talented, innovative & driven people, making meaningful connections with like-minded creatives along the way!

Role

The UGC Coordinator plays a key role in bringing creator campaigns to life. They work closely with UGC Managers to execute the day-to-day tasks that keep campaigns running—sourcing, outreach, follow-ups, content tracking, and more.

As execution-focused team members, they ensure no details are missed, timelines are met, and communication with creators stays consistent and organized.

This is a full-time, fully remote position that allows work from anywhere/everywhere in the world.

What you’ll do

 - Support the execution of UGC campaigns from creator sourcing to content delivery

 - Search for, vet, and reach out to creators based on defined criteria

 - Manage day-to-day communication with creators, including follow-ups and coordination

 - Track creator progress and ensure deliverables are completed on time

 - Flag delays, no-shows, or communication issues early and proactively

 - Keep UGC Managers updated on progress, blockers, and campaign status

 - Review content to ensure it meets basic requirements before escalation

 - Maintain and update internal systems (ClickUp, production boards, databases)

 - Support creator onboarding, agreements, and payment tracking

 - Work closely with and support UGC Managers to ensure strong campaign outcomes and timely delivery


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR (MUST HAVES)

 - Strong written and verbal English communication skills

 - Prior experience in UGC or influencer marketing campaigns is highly preferable

 - Tech-savvy and quick to adapt to new tools and platforms

 - Proactive, self-motivated, and comfortable working independently

 - Strong team player with excellent interpersonal and collaboration skills

 - Interest in influencer marketing, UGC, or digital content

 - Highly organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple tasks at once

 - Able to follow processes while also flagging issues or asking for clarification when needed

 - Reliable and consistent in delivering high-quality work in a fast-paced environment

While not required, it’s an added plus if you also have:

 - Familiarity with tools like ClickUp, Frame.io http://Frame.io, or similar

 - Experience in fast-paced agency or startup environments

 - Experience supporting campaign execution or operations roles

Why join us?

 - Unlimited Time Off & Sick Days

 - Flexible hours

 - Fully remote (with the possibility to work from anywhere)

source_jobs.title: Performance Creative | Designer & Video Editor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

We’re a tight-knit team of creative, data-driven people, continuously building services & partnering with the world’s largest brands.

We value great work with talented, innovative & driven people, making meaningful connections with like-minded creatives along the way!



Role
We’re looking for a Senior Integrated Creative who can seamlessly blend video editing and graphic design to create high-performing, on-brand campaign assets. This is a hands-on role for a highly skilled maker who thrives across both motion and static creative, someone who can take raw footage, brand guidelines, and a creative brief and turn them into polished, performance-driven content from concept to delivery.



You’ll own the full creative process, producing video content, thumbnails, social graphics, banners, and supporting assets that work together as a cohesive campaign. The ideal candidate combines exceptional craft, strong creative judgment, and a deep understanding of content built to perform across social, digital, and paid media channels.




What You’ll Do

Video editing and sequencing: Edit short-form (TikTok, Reels, Shorts) and long-form (YouTube) footage into finished cuts, from selects through to final export. Sequence, pace, and structure raw footage into a narrative that holds attention in the first three seconds and lands the message. Colour grade for consistency across a campaign and across delivery formats and platforms. Source and integrate stock footage where original material falls short. Apply sound design, music, and audio levelling so the edit feels finished, not assembled.


Graphic and static design: Design thumbnails, social graphics, digital banners, and ad creative that pair with the video work. Apply typography, colour, layout, and hierarchy to a professional standard across formats and aspect ratios. Adapt a single concept into a full set of platform-native sizes and variants without losing fidelity. Conceptualise assets from a brief, not only execute against a locked spec.


Motion design and the bridge between still and moving: Build motion graphics, animated text, lower thirds, and transitions in After Effects. Carry graphic design principles (type, colour, layout) into the video timeline so static and motion feel like one system. Produce simple VFX, masks, and overlays where the edit calls for them. Animate static brand assets into motion treatments and back again, keeping the look coherent in both states.


Brand continuity and creative systems: Keep fonts, colours, logo usage, and visual treatment consistent across every video edit and static asset for a given client. Work from and contribute to brand kits, templates, and reusable project files that speed up the next round. Catch and fix off-brand drift before it ships, across both disciplines.


Performance creative collaboration: Partner with paid media and strategy on creative built to be tested, iterated, and scaled. Produce variants and iterations quickly in response to performance data, not only one hero asset. Turn around quick-cycle edits using lightweight tools where speed beats polish.


Craft leadership and mentorship: Set the quality bar for integrated creative work and hold it under deadline pressure. Review and uplift the work of junior designers and editors, giving specific, actionable feedback. Document repeatable approaches (project file setup, export presets, template systems) so quality does not live only in one person’s head.



What we’re looking for

 - A portfolio that proves both halves: finished video work (short and long form) and static design work (thumbnails, social, ad creative), not one discipline with the other bolted on.

 - Real fluency in typography, colour, layout, and hierarchy, and the discipline to carry it into a video timeline.

 - Demonstrated ability to hold brand consistency across both video and static output.

 - Self-directed in a remote-first team; clear, low-friction communicator who delivers without hand-

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Remote, work from anywhere on a North American timezone. Competitive base plus uncapped commission.



THE SHORT VERSION

We are hiring our first Account Executive. This is a foundational sales role, not a seat in a machine. You would own inbound from senior marketers around the world, close deals, and help build our outbound motion from the ground up. Uncapped commission. Real ownership from day one. Direct access to the founders.

If you perform, you grow fast.



ABOUT INBEAT (PART OF FIELDTRIP)

Most brands work with a handful of agencies. One for paid media, one for content, one for influencer, one for SEO. The problem is they never talk to each other. Learnings get lost. Budget gets wasted. The brand ends up doing all the coordinating.

Fieldtrip fixes that.

We are a collective of specialist agencies, influencer marketing, performance creative, paid media, and SEO, working as one team. One point of contact. One shared strategy. What works in paid shapes creative the same week. What works in search informs content strategy. Everything connects.



THE ROLE

We are building our sales team and this is a foundational hire.

You would handle inbound leads, running discovery, scoping, and closing deals with senior marketers globally. We are also looking for someone who understands outbound and can help us build that motion over time.

This is consultative work. You are not order-taking. You are helping senior marketers understand why the way they currently work is not serving them, and why Fieldtrip is the smarter alternative.

There is a real career path here, not a box to sit in.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Handle and close inbound leads across global markets.

 - Run discovery, scoping, and closing calls with senior marketing decision-makers.

 - Match each prospect to the right Fieldtrip solution across influencer, paid media, creative, and SEO.

 - Help build outbound pipeline using tools like Apollo and similar outreach platforms.

 - Build and manage cadences and sequences as the outbound motion develops.

 - Use technology, automation, and AI to streamline your workflow and work efficiently.

 - Keep the CRM accurate and the pipeline clean at all times.


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 4 to 6 years of sales experience in an agency, media, or marketing technology environment.

 - A strong understanding of paid media, performance creative, and influencer marketing.

 - A proven track record of hitting and exceeding targets. We will ask for numbers.

 - Comfort running a full sales cycle from first call to close.

 - Hands-on experience with sales tools such as HubSpot, Apollo, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator.

 - The ability to build outreach cadences and sequences. A strong advantage.

 - Comfort using AI and automation to work smarter and faster.

 - A sharp, clear communicator, on calls and over email.

 - Self-directed, hungry, and comfortable in a fast-moving environment.


WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT

 - You have sold into marketing or growth teams at DTC, e-commerce, or app and subscription companies.

 - You understand performance creative, and why brands struggle to produce content at the volume paid media demands.

 - You have a genuine point of view on why the current agency model is broken.

 - You have helped build sales processes, not just followed them.

 - You use AI to research, personalise, and move faster without losing quality.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive base salary plus uncapped commission.

 - Unlimited PTO.

 - Fully remote. Work from anywhere on a North American timezone.

 - An early and influential seat as we continue to build the sales function. Real ownership from day one.

 - Direct collaboration with senior leadership and Ffounders.

 - Exposure across the full Fieldtrip ecosystem: influencer, paid media, creative, and SEO.

source_jobs.title: Creative Lead (Performance & Strategy)
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/5fce1e15-71f1-4704-a4f3-daac73a8c5e4/5c8ab980-6de0-485d-83bb-f3d7868cc614/Creative_Milkshake%20copy.png]


WHO WE ARE

Creative Milkshake is a remote-first performance creative agency. We make scroll-stopping short-form ads for some of the world’s most exciting DTC brands on Meta and TikTok - high-volume, fast-moving, and built to convert.



We’re a close-knit team of ~34 people spread across the Netherlands, the Philippines, South Africa, Spain, and beyond. We’ve recently joined the FieldTrip group alongside inBeat, which means we’re growing - in ambition, in capability, and in the complexity of clients we work with.



Our Amsterdam studio is the creative heart of our production operation. Over the next 1–2 years, we’re expanding from UGC-first content toward elevated branded formats, this role will form part of that journey.



 


THE ROLE

We are searching for a performance-focused maven with deep social roots who understands the intricacies of production and ad creation. This individual will master creative strategy, social analytics, and creative direction of both actual production (shoots) and post (editing) to lead the agency's approval process. While our Strategy Team Lead manages daily strategist operations, we require a specialized role with direction that goes beyond standard management.



The ideal candidate serves as the ultimate bridge within the studio. You possess the social expertise to craft compelling briefs, the technical production knowledge to ensure they are executable, and the refined aesthetic judgment to distinguish exceptional ads from merely good ones. More importantly, you can provide the actionable feedback necessary to elevate the team's work.



Operating at the nexus of platform intelligence, production, and strategy, you will act as our creative conscience. Your mission is to establish high standards, develop the systems that maintain them, and guarantee that every piece of content produced by Creative Milkshake is a source of collective pride.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO


OWN CREATIVE QUALITY, END TO END

 - Set and hold the creative standard for all output - from storyboard to final ad

 - Review and give specific, actionable feedback on Creative Strategy Presentations (CSPs), storyboards, RAW footage, and final ads before they reach clients

 - Build and maintain the checklists, brand guides, and review frameworks that keep quality consistent as the team grows

 - Develop an ad example library and “what makes a great UGC ad” resource - codifying instinct into something the whole team can learn from


BE THE BRIDGE BETWEEN STRATEGY AND PRODUCTION

 - Translate creative briefs into production-executable direction, and translate what’s possible on set back into strategy

 - Run pre-production alignment sessions between Creative Strategists and Producers so we go into every shoot prepared, not reactive

 - Identify where handoffs break down and build the processes to fix them

 - Be the person each department comes to when they’re not sure what the other team needs


DRIVE PLATFORM & TREND INTELLIGENCE

 - Maintain a genuine, current pulse on social platforms (e.g. TikTok and Meta) - what formats are performing, what hooks are landing, what’s oversaturated

 - Feed trend intelligence to the Creative Strategy team in a way that’s actionable, not just inspirational

 - Bring a production lens to trends: not just “what’s working” but “how do we make that in our studio?

 - Use AI tools as a natural part of ideation, scripting, and research workflows


SUPPORT KEY CLIENT CREATIVE RELATIONSHIPS

 - Take the lead on creative input for a subset of VIP or complex client accounts

 - Join brainstorming and kick-off sessions where the creative direction needs a senior voice

 - Help the accounts team set accurate creative expectations with clients upfront - reducing revision cycles before they’ve started


REPRESENT CREATIVE MILKSHAKE EXTERNALLY

 - Own appro

source_jobs.title: Video Editor & Designer
source_jobs.description:
[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/5fce1e15-71f1-4704-a4f3-daac73a8c5e4/eebc693c-5d5a-4e2c-bccf-6d0020e20877/Creative_Milkshake%20copy.png]




ABOUT THE ROLE

This role is for our partner agency Creative Milkshake - a performance creative agency working with the world's coolest DTC brands. We create social thumb-stopping ads that are designed to convert - that's our jam.

A Video Editor & Designer at Creative Milkshake is responsible for turning creative briefs and storyboards into high-performing short-form and long-form video content. You’ll bring concepts to life through strong pacing, storytelling, and visual execution, ensuring every asset is on-brand, and delivered with precision in a fast-moving production environment.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create short-form and long-form videos from creative briefs & storyboards for social media, ads, and digital campaigns (Reels, TikTok, YouTube, Meta, etc.)

 - Create supporting graphic assets (thumbnails, overlays, end cards, captions)

 - Apply strong pacing, storytelling, and visual hierarchy

 - Follow brand guidelines and implement feedback accurately

 - Manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment

 - Work closely with creative strategists, producers, and team leads

 - Organize project files and maintain clean, scalable workflows


SKILLS

 - Expertise in professional editing software
   
   - Adobe Premiere Pro
   
   - Adobe After Effects (basic motion & text animation) is a plus

 - Strong design fundamentals (typography, layout, composition)

 - Ability to enhance edits with color correction and audio-sweetening techniques, i.e, background music and SFX

 - Familiarity with AI-assisted tools (Runway, Pika, Midjourney, etc.)

 - Attention to detail and ability to work fast without sacrificing quality


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Standard Creative Milkshake Stipends (mental health, healthcare & internet)

 - Remote-first & flexible work culture

 - Generous PTO

 - A collaborative, creative, ridiculously fun team

 - Learning & career development

notes: Mostly sales and marketing roles, with a few creative and operations positions. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
